title: ADR-005: credit() must floor balance at zero — driver divergence on refund clawback
content: medical-bill-negotiator stabilization-triage highest-priority defect (verified against live code 2026-06-01): the Polar refund handler (src/app/api/polar/webhook/route.ts:51) calls credit(userId, -b.tokens, 'refund', `refund:`) with NO zero floor. Both Store drivers compute next = cur + amount and write it. DIVERGENCE: PGlite token_accounts has `constraint balance_non_negative check (balance >= 0)` (pglite-store.ts:38-39) so a refund when cur webhook (no try/catch around credit) 500s -> Polar retry storm. Firestore (PROD) has NO such constraint -> balance silently goes negative -> every future charge reads balance

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 13:47 UTC
SDLC team chains route backend ADR handoffs to Visual Brand Asset Factory by mistake
content: On the 'SDLC — Medical Bill Negotiator' team, a chain (source persona T: Solution Architect, 300e2d99) emitted a team_handoff whose payload was ADR-005 (token credit() non-negative balance floor — a backend money-path defect scoped for Dev Clone). It was routed to T: Visual Brand Asset Factory (7b95ec9e), which has no matching trigger: brand-asset generation needs a manual asset request or a brand.brief.generated event plus a design.md brief, and image/vision vault credentials (aq_image_model / aq_vision_model). None were present — only the Codebase connector. Correct behavior on such mis-routed code-defect handoffs: do NOT fabricate asset work; report precondition_failed and note the handoff belongs to Dev Clone. The brand-asset persona is not a step in the architecture.analysis.completed → implement → PR → QA → review chain.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 13:47 UTC
ADR-005 credit() floor: handed to Code Reviewer before Dev Clone implemented — INCOMPLETE
content: medical-bill-negotiator, 2026-06-01: team_handoff routed ADR-005 (token credit() non-negative floor) to Code Reviewer, but the engineer (Dev Clone) step had NOT run. Verified against live code: pglite-store.ts:196 still `const next = num(cur.rows[0]?.balance) + amount` and firestore-store.ts:127 still `const next = cur + amount` — NO Math.max(0,...) clamp; named deliverable tests/store-credit.test.ts does NOT exist; store files last modified May 31 13:01, before ADR-005 authored Jun 1 15:46. Verdict REQUEST_CHANGES/INCOMPLETE, business_outcome=no_input_available. The DEFECT ITSELF is real+unfixed and confirmed: webhook route.ts:51 calls credit(userId,-b.tokens,'refund',...) with no try/catch (only try/catch guards signature validation lines 18-23); PGlite CHECK balance>=0 (line 39) throws on sub-zero refund -> 500 -> Polar retry storm; Firestore (prod) has no constraint -> silent negative balance -> charge refuses forever. RECURRING PATTERN (3rd time, see ADR-001 bill-diff): architect ADRs are being chained to Code Reviewer as if they were changesets. Policy reaffirmed: an ADR is an input to the engineer, not a reviewable change — verify target symbols/files exist first; if absent, REQUEST_CHANGES/INCOMPLETE, never fabricate findings, never APPROVE. Routing fix: architecture.analysis.completed should go to Dev Clone; re-trigger review on implementation.completed/dev-clone.pr.created. Also adjacent: large uncommitted Supabase->Firestore/PGlite migration in working tree needs its own dedicated review.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 13:49 UTC
Security re-scan 2026-06-01: NEW HIGH PHI egress to Langfuse Cloud (2nd sink); all baseline findings still open
content:
Re-scan of medical-bill-negotiator. 0 Critical, 2 High, 2 Medium, 2 Low. ZERO baseline findings fixed; ONE NEW High.

NEW [HIGH][open] phi-egress-langfuse: commit a1bb49e added withTracing wrapper — src/lib/llm/index.ts:41,44,55,58 call recordLlmCall({input: input.prompt}) and observability.ts:66 sends `input: t.input` (full unredacted prompt = PHI: patient name/account/provider/charges) to Langfuse CLOUD for every LLM call. LANGFUSE_* keys ARE configured so it is LIVE. This is a SECOND external PHI egress sink stacked on top of the existing LightTrack one. No redaction, no BAA/consent gate. Single choke point to gate = observability.ts recordLlmCall. Fix: strip prompt body, send only operation+token-counts+model.

STILL OPEN (unchanged): [HIGH] phi-egress-lighttrack gemini.ts:40 + claude-cli.ts:88 (input: content/prompt to LightTrack); [MED] no-security-headers next.config.mjs still has no headers() (only serverExternalPackages); [MED] 10 moderate npm advisories now via firebase-admin->@google-cloud/firestore->google-gax->teeny-request/retry-request->uuid chain + postcss<8.5.10 — npm audit fix --force still proposes semver-major DOWNGRADES (false fixes); [LOW] claude-cli spawn shell:true dev-only.

VERIFIED CLEAN re-confirmed: no hardcoded secrets in src, SQL parameterized, auth verifies idToken+checkRevoked, webhook validates signatures, .env.local gitignored (git check-ignore ok). DOMAIN LESSON reinforced: every new observability/logging feature on this PHI app is a recurring egress risk — always trace where prompt `content`/`input.prompt` flows. Each new LLM-tracing integration adds a PHI sink unless redaction is built in first.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 13:51 UTC
Release no-op 2026-06-01: handoff from Security Sentinel ≠ merge; HEAD already at v0.3.0
content:
Release Automation correctly NO-OPPED. Triggered by a team_handoff from Security Sentinel (read-only scan), not a merge. Verified: package.json=0.3.0; git log v0.3.0..HEAD empty (HEAD d4d4cc4 IS the tagged commit); CHANGELOG [Unreleased] empty. No committed merge to classify → no bump, no changelog/tag change.

RULE REINFORCED: a release requires committed/merged changes. Read-only handoffs (security scans, reviews) and uncommitted working-tree edits are NOT releasable — do not cut a release off them. Don't fabricate a bump to satisfy the trigger.

PENDING for NEXT release: working tree (uncommitted) holds a Supabase→Firestore auth migration (added src/lib/firestore/, firestore-store.ts, pglite-store.ts, lighttrack.ts; deleted supabase/*, db/pg.ts, db/sqlite.ts) + token/auth edits. When committed/merged, classify then — likely 0.4.0 MINOR (platform/feature, backward-compatible) per pre-1.0 convention; 0.3.1 PATCH if fix-only. Security Sentinel's open HIGH PHI-egress fixes (Langfuse/LightTrack) on their own = PATCH.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 13:53 UTC
Docs sync 2026-06-01: synced through dcdff6d (post-v0.3.0); README Testing drift fixed
content: Counterbill / Medical Bill Negotiator docs sync. Advanced last-processed 2f027a3→dcdff6d. Release Manager's v0.3.0 CHANGELOG body was already accurate — did NOT rewrite it. Two doc-drift fixes: (1) CHANGELOG [Unreleased]→Fixed = the credit()-floor token-refund fix (dcdff6d, the single commit after the v0.3.0 tag) — refunds now clamp balance at zero in both store drivers, fixing a Polar-webhook 500/retry-storm (PGlite) and silent-negative-balance (Firestore prod). (2) README 'Testing' section falsely claimed 'No automated test suite is wired' — but vitest (test: vitest run) + 10 tests/ files shipped across v0.3.0; updated to document npm test. RULE: README + CHANGELOG move together; only document COMMITTED functional changes. DEFERRED (uncommitted working tree): Supabase→Firestore auth migration — store.ts dispatch already swapped pg/sqlite→firestore/pglite in dcdff6d, but env/config unmerged, so README tech-stack/env surfaces left untouched until it lands as a merge.
